hawkish_phrases: monetary policy could need to be tightened by a somewhat greater extent over the forecast period
inflation is forecast to remain above the MPC's target throughout the forecast period
there are limits to the extent to which above-target inflation can be tolerated
In the final year of the forecast, however, the output gap closes and inflation rises slightly further above the target
Kristin Forbes considered it appropriate to increase Bank Rate by 25 basis points
dovish_phrases: Aggregate demand slowed markedly in 2017 Q1
consumption growth will be slower in the near term than previously anticipated
wage growth has been notably weaker than expected
Attempting to offset fully the effect of weaker sterling on inflation would be achievable only at the cost of higher unemployment
summary: The MPC voted 7-1 to hold Bank Rate at 0.25% with one dissenter (Forbes) voting for a 25bp hike, and provided explicit forward guidance that policy could need to be tightened by a somewhat greater extent than market expectations, signaling a clear hawkish bias despite the hold.
